content:
type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add 3 new methods, each right after its sibling resolve_* method, in the same 3 classes:

1) In ImageBlock (after resolve_image, ~line 126): 
def resolve_image_with_size(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it, and also return its byte size."""
    buffer = self.resolve_image(as_base64=as_base64)
    size = len(buffer.getvalue())
    buffer.seek(0)
    return buffer, size

2) In AudioBlock (after resolve_audio, ~line 186):
def resolve_audio_with_size(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """Resolve audio such that it can be read, and also return its byte size."""
    buffer = self.resolve_audio(as_base64=as_base64)
    size = len(buffer.getvalue())
    buffer.seek(0)
    return buffer, size

3) In DocumentBlock (after resolve_document, ~line 223):
def resolve_document_with_size(self) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """Resolve a document such that it is represented by a BytesIO object, and also return its byte size."""
    buffer = self.resolve_document()
    size = len(buffer.getvalue())
    buffer.seek(0)
    return buffer, size

Notes: use  already imported. Return type is Tuple[BytesIO, int] (use  from typing if that's the style already used in file, otherwise builtin tuple is fine - check existing style in the file). Buffer must be left seeked to position 0 (start) so callers can read the full content, since getvalue() doesn't move the cursor but resolve_binary may have left the cursor elsewhere. These must just wrap the existing resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document (calling them internally) so they automatically inherit your zero-byte validation changes. Original resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document methods must remain otherwise unchanged in behavior/signature (aside from your zero-byte validation).
